full_response:
We wish to compute the area of a “geodesic circle” of radius R in each 2‐dimensional space, i.e. the set of all points at geodesic distance ≤R from the origin.  

1.  Euclidean plane, metric  
   ds² = dr² + r² dφ²,    0≤r≤R,  0≤φ<2π.  
   
   The area element is  
     dA = √det[g]\,dr\,dφ = r dr dφ.  
   Hence  
     A_E(R) = ∫_{φ=0}^{2π} ∫_{r=0}^{R} r dr dφ  
            = 2π ∫_{0}^{R} r dr  
            = 2π · ½R²  
            = π R².  

2.  Lobachevsky (constant negative curvature) plane, metric  
   ds² = du² + cosh²(u)\,dv²,    0≤u≤U,  0≤v<2π.  
   
   Here u itself is the radial (geodesic) distance from the origin.  The area element is  
     dA = √det[g]\,du\,dv = cosh(u)\,du\,dv.  
   Thus  
     A_H(U) = ∫_{v=0}^{2π} ∫_{u=0}^{U} cosh(u)\,du\,dv  
            = 2π ∫_{0}^{U} cosh(u)\,du  
            = 2π [ sinh(u) ]_{0}^{U}  
            = 2π sinh(U).  

Final boxed answer:

\boxed{  
A_{\rm Euclid}(R)=\pi\,R^{2},  
\quad  
A_{\rm Lobachevsky}(U)=2\pi\,\sinh(U)\,.  
}

Confidence: 100%
